 601→    private fun openOrToggleMicToolbar() {
 602→        if (!canRecordAudio(this)) {
 603→            ActivityCompat.requestPermissions(
 604→                this,
 605→                arrayOf(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO),
 606→                REQUEST_AUDIO_PERMISSION
 607→            )
 608→        } else {
 609→            toggleMicToolBar()
 610→        }
 611→    }
 612→
 613→    private fun toggleMicToolBar() {
 614→        audioView?.let {
 615→            it.visibility = if (it.visibility != View.VISIBLE) View.VISIBLE else View.GONE
 616→            return
 617→        }
 618→        // Record mic tool bar does not exist yet
 619→        tempAudioPath = generateTempAudioFile(this)
 620→        if (tempAudioPath == null) {
 621→            return
 622→        }
 623→        audioView = createRecorderInstance(
 624→            this,
 625→            R.drawable.ic_play_arrow_white_24dp,
 626→            R.drawable.ic_pause_white_24dp,
 627→            R.drawable.ic_stop_white_24dp,
 628→            R.drawable.ic_rec,
 629→            R.drawable.ic_rec_stop,
 630→            tempAudioPath!!
 631→        )
 632→        if (audioView == null) {
 633→            tempAudioPath = null
 634→            return
 635→        }
 636→        val lp2 = FrameLayout.LayoutParams(
 637→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT,
 638→            ViewGroup.LayoutParams.MATCH_PARENT
 639→        )
 640→        audioView!!.layoutParams = lp2
 641→        val micToolBarLayer = findViewById<LinearLayout>(R.id.mic_tool_bar_layer)
 642→        micToolBarLayer.addView(audioView)
 643→    }

 690→    @NeedsTest("Starting animation from swipe is inverse to the finishing one")

 692→        if (currentCard == null) {
 693→            showSnackbar(getString(R.string.multimedia_editor_something_wrong), Snackbar.LENGTH_SHORT)
 694→            return
 695→        }
 696→        val intent = if (BackendFactory.defaultLegacySchema) {
 697→            Intent(this, CardInfo::class.java).apply {
 698→                putExtra("cardId", currentCard!!.id)
 699→            }
 700→        } else {
 701→            com.ichi2.anki.pages.CardInfo.getIntent(this, currentCard!!.id)
 702→        }
 703→        val animation = getAnimationTransitionFromGesture(fromGesture)
 704→        intent.putExtra(FINISH_ANIMATION_EXTRA, getInverseTransition(animation) as Parcelable)
 705→        startActivityWithAnimation(intent, animation)
 706→    }

 708→    // Related to https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/11061#issuecomment-1107868455
 709→    @NeedsTest("Order of operations needs Testing around Menu (Overflow) Icons and their colors.")
 710→    override fun onCreateOptionsMenu(menu: Menu): Boolean {
 711→        Timber.d("onCreateOptionsMenu()")
 712→        // NOTE: This is called every time a new question is shown via invalidate options menu
 713→        menuInflater.inflate(R.menu.reviewer, menu)
 714→        displayIcons(menu)
 715→        mActionButtons.setCustomButtonsStatus(menu)
 716→        var alpha = if (super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 717→        val markCardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_mark_card)
 718→        if (currentCard != null && isMarked(currentCard!!.note())) {
 719→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_unmark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_white)
 720→        } else {
 721→            markCardIcon.setTitle(R.string.menu_mark_note).setIcon(R.drawable.ic_star_border_white)
 722→        }
 723→        markCardIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 724→
 725→        val flagIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_flag)
 726→        if (flagIcon != null) {
 727→            if (currentCard != null) {
 728→                when (currentCard!!.userFlag()) {
 729→                    1 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_red)
 730→                    2 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_orange)
 731→                    3 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_green)
 732→                    4 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_blue)
 733→                    5 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_pink)
 734→                    6 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_turquoise)
 735→                    7 -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_purple)
 736→                    else -> flagIcon.setIcon(R.drawable.ic_flag_transparent)
 737→                }
 738→            }
 739→            flagIcon.iconAlpha = alpha
 740→        }
 741→
 742→        // Undo button
 743→        @DrawableRes val undoIconId: Int
 744→        val undoEnabled: Boolean
 745→        val whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes = mShowWhiteboard && whiteboard?.undoEmpty() == false
 746→        if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {
 747→            undoIconId = R.drawable.eraser
 748→            undoEnabled = true
 749→        } else {
 750→            undoIconId = R.drawable.ic_undo_white
 751→            undoEnabled = colIsOpen() && col.undoAvailable()
 752→        }
 753→        val alphaUndo = if (undoEnabled && super.controlBlocked !== ReviewerUi.ControlBlock.SLOW) Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT else Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 754→        val undoIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_undo)
 755→        undoIcon.setIcon(undoIconId)
 756→        undoIcon.setEnabled(undoEnabled).iconAlpha = alphaUndo
 757→        undoIcon.actionView!!.isEnabled = undoEnabled
 758→        if (colIsOpen()) { // Required mostly because there are tests where `col` is null
 759→            if (whiteboardIsShownAndHasStrokes) {
 760→                undoIcon.title = resources.getString(R.string.undo_action_whiteboard_last_stroke)
 761→            } else if (col.undoAvailable()) {
 762→                undoIcon.title = resources.getString(R.string.studyoptions_congrats_undo, col.undoName(resources))
 763→                //  e.g. Undo Bury, Undo Change Deck, Undo Update Note
 764→            } else {
 765→                // In this case, there is no object word for the verb, "Undo",
 766→                // so in some languages such as Japanese, which have pre/post-positional particle with the object,
 767→                // we need to use the string for just "Undo" instead of the string for "Undo %s".
 768→                undoIcon.title = resources.getString(R.string.undo)
 769→            }
 770→        }
 771→        if (undoEnabled) {
 772→            mOnboarding.onUndoButtonEnabled()
 773→        }
 774→        val toggleWhiteboardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_toggle_whiteboard)
 775→        val toggleStylusIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_toggle_stylus)
 776→        val hideWhiteboardIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_hide_whiteboard)
 777→        val changePenColorIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_change_whiteboard_pen_color)
 778→        // White board button
 779→        if (prefWhiteboard) {
 780→            // Configure the whiteboard related items in the action bar
 781→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.disable_whiteboard)
 782→            // Always allow "Disable Whiteboard", even if "Enable Whiteboard" is disabled
 783→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.isVisible = true
 784→            if (!mActionButtons.status.toggleStylusIsDisabled()) {
 785→                toggleStylusIcon.isVisible = true
 786→            }
 787→            if (!mActionButtons.status.hideWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 788→                hideWhiteboardIcon.isVisible = true
 789→            }
 790→            if (!mActionButtons.status.clearWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 791→                menu.findItem(R.id.action_clear_whiteboard).isVisible = true
 792→            }
 793→            if (!mActionButtons.status.saveWhiteboardIsDisabled()) {
 794→                menu.findItem(R.id.action_save_whiteboard).isVisible = true
 795→            }
 796→            if (!mActionButtons.status.whiteboardPenColorIsDisabled()) {
 797→                changePenColorIcon.isVisible = true
 798→            }
 799→            val whiteboardIcon = ContextCompat.getDrawable(this, R.drawable.ic_gesture_white)!!.mutate()
 800→            val stylusIcon = ContextCompat.getDrawable(this, R.drawable.ic_gesture_stylus)!!.mutate()
 801→            val whiteboardColorPaletteIcon = VectorDrawableCompat.create(resources, R.drawable.ic_color_lens_white_24dp, null)!!.mutate()
 802→            if (mShowWhiteboard) {
 803→                whiteboardIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 804→                hideWhiteboardIcon.icon = whiteboardIcon
 805→                hideWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.hide_whiteboard)
 806→                whiteboardColorPaletteIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 807→                changePenColorIcon.icon = whiteboardColorPaletteIcon
 808→                if (toggleStylus) {
 809→                    toggleStylusIcon.setTitle(R.string.disable_stylus)
 810→                    stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_ENABLED_LIGHT
 811→                } else {
 812→                    toggleStylusIcon.setTitle(R.string.enable_stylus)
 813→                    stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 814→                }
 815→                toggleStylusIcon.icon = stylusIcon
 816→            } else {
 817→                whiteboardIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 818→                hideWhiteboardIcon.icon = whiteboardIcon
 819→                hideWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.show_whiteboard)
 820→                whiteboardColorPaletteIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 821→                stylusIcon.alpha = Themes.ALPHA_ICON_DISABLED_LIGHT
 822→                toggleStylusIcon.isEnabled = false
 823→                toggleStylusIcon.icon = stylusIcon
 824→                changePenColorIcon.isEnabled = false
 825→                changePenColorIcon.icon = whiteboardColorPaletteIcon
 826→                mColorPalette.visibility = View.GONE
 827→            }
 828→        } else {
 829→            toggleWhiteboardIcon.setTitle(R.string.enable_whiteboard)
 830→        }
 831→        if (colIsOpen() && col.decks.isDyn(parentDid)) {
 832→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_open_deck_options).isVisible = false
 833→        }
 834→        if (mTTS.enabled && !mActionButtons.status.selectTtsIsDisabled()) {
 835→            menu.findItem(R.id.action_select_tts).isVisible = true
 836→        }
 837→        // Setup bury / suspend providers
 838→        val suspendIcon = menu.findItem(R.id.action_suspend)

 917→    override fun displayAnswerBottomBar() {
 918→        super.displayAnswerBottomBar()
 919→        mOnboarding.onAnswerShown()
 920→        val buttonCount: Int = try {
 921→            this.buttonCount
 922→        } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
 923→            CrashReportService.sendExceptionReport(e, "AbstractReviewer-showEaseButtons")
 924→            closeReviewer(DeckPicker.RESULT_DB_ERROR, true)

 926→        }

 928→        // Set correct label and background resource for each button
 929→        // Note that it's necessary to set the resource dynamically as the ease2 / ease3 buttons
 930→        // (which libanki expects ease to be 2 and 3) can either be hard, good, or easy - depending on num buttons shown
 931→        val background = getBackgroundColors(this)
 932→        val textColor = getTextColors(this)
 933→        easeButton1!!.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
 934→        easeButton1!!.setColor(background[0])
 935→        easeButton4!!.setColor(background[3])
 936→        when (buttonCount) {
 937→            2 -> {
 938→                // Ease 2 is "good"
 939→                easeButton2!!.setup(background[2], textColor[2], R.string.ease_button_good)
 940→                easeButton2!!.requestFocus()
 941→            }
 942→            3 -> {
 943→                // Ease 2 is good
 944→                easeButton2!!.setup(background[2], textColor[2], R.string.ease_button_good)
 945→                // Ease 3 is easy
 946→                easeButton3!!.setup(background[3], textColor[3], R.string.ease_button_easy)
 947→                easeButton2!!.requestFocus()
 948→            }
 949→            else -> {
 950→                // Ease 2 is "hard"
 951→                easeButton2!!.setup(background[1], textColor[1], R.string.ease_button_hard)
 952→                easeButton2!!.requestFocus()
 953→                // Ease 3 is good
 954→                easeButton3!!.setup(background[2], textColor[2], R.string.ease_button_good)
 955→                easeButton4!!.setVisibility(View.VISIBLE)
 956→                easeButton3!!.requestFocus()
 957→            }
 958→        }
 959→
 960→        // Show next review time
 961→        if (shouldShowNextReviewTime()) {
 962→            fun nextIvlStr(button: Int) = sched!!.nextIvlStr(this, currentCard!!, button)
 963→
 964→            easeButton1!!.nextTime = nextIvlStr(Consts.BUTTON_ONE)
 965→            easeButton2!!.nextTime = nextIvlStr(Consts.BUTTON_TWO)

 968→            }
 969→            if (buttonCount > 3) {
 970→                easeButton4!!.nextTime = nextIvlStr(Consts.BUTTON_FOUR)

1021→    private fun updateScreenCounts() {
1022→        if (currentCard == null) return
1023→        super.updateActionBar()
1024→        val actionBar = supportActionBar
1025→        val counts = sched!!.counts(currentCard!!)
1026→        if (actionBar != null) {
1027→            if (mPrefShowETA) {
1028→                mEta = sched!!.eta(counts, false)
1029→                actionBar.subtitle = Utils.remainingTime(this, (mEta * 60).toLong())
1030→            }
1031→        }
1032→        mNewCount = SpannableString(counts.new.toString())
1033→        mLrnCount = SpannableString(counts.lrn.toString())
1034→        mRevCount = SpannableString(counts.rev.toString())
1035→        if (mPrefHideDueCount) {
1036→            mRevCount = SpannableString("???")
1037→        }
1038→        // if this code is run as a card is being answered, currentCard may be non-null but
1039→        // the queues may be empty - we can't call countIdx() in such a case
1040→        if (counts.count() != 0) {
1041→            when (sched!!.countIdx(currentCard!!)) {
1042→                Counts.Queue.NEW -> mNewCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mNewCount!!.length, 0)
1043→                Counts.Queue.LRN -> mLrnCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mLrnCount!!.length, 0)
1044→                Counts.Queue.REV -> mRevCount!!.setSpan(UnderlineSpan(), 0, mRevCount!!.length, 0)
1045→            }
1046→        }
1047→        mTextBarNew.text = mNewCount
1048→        mTextBarLearn.text = mLrnCount
1049→        mTextBarReview.text = mRevCount
1050→    }

1125→        }
1126→        when (which) {
1127→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_RED -> {
1128→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_RED)
1129→                return true
1130→            }
1131→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE -> {
1132→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_ORANGE)
1133→                return true
1134→            }
1135→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN -> {
1136→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_GREEN)
1137→                return true
1138→            }
1139→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE -> {
1140→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_BLUE)
1141→                return true
1142→            }
1143→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK -> {
1144→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PINK)
1145→                return true
1146→            }
1147→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE -> {
1148→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_TURQUOISE)
1149→                return true
1150→            }
1151→            ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE -> {
1152→                toggleFlag(CardMarker.FLAG_PURPLE)
1153→                return true
1154→            }
1155→            ViewerCommand.UNSET_FLAG -> {
1156→                onFlag(currentCard, CardMarker.FLAG_NONE)
1157→                return true
1158→            }
1159→            ViewerCommand.MARK -> {
1160→                onMark(currentCard)
1161→                return true

1163→            ViewerCommand.ADD_NOTE -> {

1166→            }
1167→            ViewerCommand.CARD_INFO -> {

1170→            }
1171→            else -> return super.executeCommand(which, fromGesture)

1239→    @Suppress("deprecation") // #9332: UI Visibility -> Insets
1240→    private fun setFullScreen(a: AbstractFlashcardViewer) {
1241→        // Set appropriate flags to enable Sticky Immersive mode.
1242→        a.window.decorView.systemUiVisibility = (
1243→            View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_STABLE // | View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_HIDE_NAVIGATION // temporarily disabled due to #5245
1244→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LAYOUT_FULLSCREEN
1245→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION
1246→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_FULLSCREEN
1247→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_LOW_PROFILE
1248→                or View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_IMMERSIVE
1249→            )
1250→        // Show / hide the Action bar together with the status bar
1251→        val prefs = a.sharedPrefs()
1252→        val fullscreenMode = fromPreference(prefs)
1253→        a.window.statusBarColor = getColorFromAttr(a, android.R.attr.colorPrimary)
1254→        val decorView = a.window.decorView
1255→        decorView.setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener { flags: Int ->
1256→            val toolbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.toolbar)
1257→            val answerButtons = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.answer_options_layout)
1258→            val topbar = a.findViewById<View>(R.id.top_bar)
1259→            if (toolbar == null || topbar == null || answerButtons == null) {
1260→                return@setOnSystemUiVisibilityChangeListener
1261→            }
1262→            // Note that system bars will only be "visible" if none of the
1263→            // LOW_PROFILE, HIDE_NAVIGATION, or FULLSCREEN flags are set.
1264→            val visible = flags and View.SYSTEM_UI_FLAG_HIDE_NAVIGATION == 0
1265→            Timber.d("System UI visibility change. Visible: %b", visible)
1266→            if (visible) {
1267→                showViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1268→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1269→                    showViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1270→                    showViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1271→                }
1272→            } else {
1273→                hideViewWithAnimation(toolbar)
1274→                if (fullscreenMode == FullScreenMode.FULLSCREEN_ALL_GONE) {
1275→                    hideViewWithAnimation(topbar)
1276→                    hideViewWithAnimation(answerButtons)
1277→                }
1278→            }
1279→        }
1280→    }

1282→    private fun showViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1283→        view.alpha = 0.0f
1284→        view.visibility = View.VISIBLE
1285→        view.animate().alpha(TRANSPARENCY).setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong()).setListener(null)
1286→    }
1287→
1288→    private fun hideViewWithAnimation(view: View) {
1289→        view.animate()
1290→            .alpha(0f)
1291→            .setDuration(ANIMATION_DURATION.toLong())
1292→            .setListener(object : AnimatorListenerAdapter() {
1293→                override fun onAnimationEnd(animation: Animator) {
1294→                    view.visibility = View.GONE
1295→                }
1296→            })
1297→    }

1304→    private fun createWhiteboard() {
1305→        whiteboard = createInstance(this, true, this)
1306→
1307→        // We use the pen color of the selected deck at the time the whiteboard is enabled.
1308→        // This is how all other whiteboard settings are
1309→        val whiteboardPenColor = MetaDB.getWhiteboardPenColor(this, parentDid).fromPreferences()
1310→        if (whiteboardPenColor != null) {
1311→            whiteboard!!.penColor = whiteboardPenColor
1312→        }
1313→        whiteboard!!.setOnPaintColorChangeListener(object : OnPaintColorChangeListener {
1314→            override fun onPaintColorChange(color: Int?) {
1315→                MetaDB.storeWhiteboardPenColor(this@Reviewer, parentDid, !currentTheme.isNightMode, color)
1316→            }
1317→        })
1318→        whiteboard!!.setOnTouchListener { v: View, event: MotionEvent? ->
1319→            if (event == null) return@setOnTouchListener false
1320→            // If the whiteboard is currently drawing, and triggers the system UI to show, we want to continue drawing.
1321→            if (!whiteboard!!.isCurrentlyDrawing && (
1322→                !mShowWhiteboard || (
1323→                    mPrefFullscreenReview &&
1324→                        isImmersiveSystemUiVisible(this@Reviewer)
1325→                    )
1326→                )
1327→            ) {
1328→                // Bypass whiteboard listener when it's hidden or fullscreen immersive mode is temporarily suspended
1329→                v.performClick()
1330→                return@setOnTouchListener gestureDetector!!.onTouchEvent(event)
1331→            }
1332→            whiteboard!!.handleTouchEvent(event)
1333→        }
1334→    }

1371→    /**
1372→     * Whether or not dismiss note is available for current card and specified DismissType
1373→     * @return true if there is another card of same note that could be dismissed
1374→     */
1375→    @KotlinCleanup("mCurrentCard handling")
1376→    private fun suspendNoteAvailable(): Boolean {
1377→        return if (currentCard == null || isControlBlocked) {
1378→            false
1379→        } else {
1380→            col.db.queryScalar(
1381→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue != " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_SUSPENDED + " limit 1",
1382→                currentCard!!.nid,
1383→                currentCard!!.id
1384→            ) == 1
1385→        }
1386→        // whether there exists a sibling not buried.
1387→    }
1388→
1389→    @KotlinCleanup("mCurrentCard handling")
1390→    private fun buryNoteAvailable(): Boolean {
1391→        return if (currentCard == null || isControlBlocked) {
1392→            false
1393→        } else {
1394→            col.db.queryScalar(
1395→                "select 1 from cards where nid = ? and id != ? and queue >=  " + Consts.QUEUE_TYPE_NEW + " limit 1",
1396→                currentCard!!.nid,
1397→                currentCard!!.id
1398→            ) == 1
1399→        }
1400→        // Whether there exists a sibling which is neither suspended nor buried

1482→    private fun createActionViewWith(
1483→        context: Context,
1484→        menuItem: MenuItem,
1485→        @MenuRes subMenuRes: Int,
1486→        onMenuItemSelection: (MenuItem) -> Boolean,
1487→        showsSubMenu: () -> Boolean
1488→    ): View = ImageButton(context, null, android.R.attr.actionButtonStyle).apply {
1489→        TooltipCompat.setTooltipText(this, menuItem.title)
1490→        menuItem.icon?.isAutoMirrored = true
1491→        setImageDrawable(menuItem.icon)
1492→        id = menuItem.itemId
1493→        setOnClickListener {
1494→            if (!menuItem.isEnabled) {
1495→                return@setOnClickListener
1496→            }
1497→            if (showsSubMenu()) {
1498→                PopupMenu(context, this).apply {
1499→                    inflate(subMenuRes)
1500→                    setOnMenuItemClickListener(onMenuItemSelection)
1501→                    show()
1502→                }
1503→            } else {
1504→                onOptionsItemSelected(menuItem)
1505→            }
1506→        }
1507→    }

1613→        }

 17→package com.ichi2.anki.analytics
 18→
 19→import android.content.Context
 20→import android.content.SharedPreferences
 21→import android.os.Build
 22→import android.webkit.WebSettings
 23→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 24→import androidx.core.content.edit
 25→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalytics
 26→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.GoogleAnalyticsConfig
 27→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.httpclient.OkHttpClientImpl
 28→import com.brsanthu.googleanalytics.request.DefaultRequest
 29→import com.ichi2.anki.AnkiDroidApp
 30→import com.ichi2.anki.BuildConfig
 31→import com.ichi2.anki.R
 32→import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 33→import com.ichi2.utils.DisplayUtils
 34→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 35→import com.ichi2.utils.WebViewDebugging.hasSetDataDirectory
 36→import org.acra.ACRA
 37→import org.acra.util.Installation
 38→import timber.log.Timber
 39→
 40→@KotlinCleanup("see if we can make variables lazy, or properties without the `s` prefix")
 41→object UsageAnalytics {
 42→    const val ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY = "analyticsOptIn"
 43→
 44→    @KotlinCleanup("lateinit")
 45→    private var sAnalytics: GoogleAnalytics? = null
 46→    private var sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler: Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler? = null
 47→    private var sOptIn = false
 48→    private var sAnalyticsTrackingId: String? = null
 49→    private var sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = -1
 50→
 51→    /**
 52→     * Initialize the analytics provider - must be called prior to sending anything.
 53→     * Usage after that is static
 54→     * Note: may need to implement sampling strategy internally to limit hits, or not track Reviewer...
 55→     *
 56→     * @param context required to look up the analytics codes for the app
 57→     */
 58→    @Synchronized
 59→    fun initialize(context: Context): GoogleAnalytics? {
 60→        Timber.i("initialize()")
 61→        if (sAnalytics == null) {
 62→            Timber.d("App tracking id 'tid' = %s", getAnalyticsTag(context))
 63→            val gaConfig = GoogleAnalyticsConfig()
 64→                .setBatchingEnabled(true)
 65→                .setSamplePercentage(getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context))
 66→                .setBatchSize(1) // until this handles application termination we will lose hits if batch>1
 67→            sAnalytics = GoogleAnalytics.builder()
 68→                .withTrackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 69→                .withConfig(gaConfig)
 70→                .withDefaultRequest(
 71→                    AndroidDefaultRequest()
 72→                        .setAndroidRequestParameters(context)
 73→                        .applicationName(context.getString(R.string.app_name))
 74→                        .applicationVersion(Integer.toString(BuildConfig.VERSION_CODE))
 75→                        .applicationId(BuildConfig.APPLICATION_ID)
 76→                        .trackingId(getAnalyticsTag(context))
 77→                        .clientId(Installation.id(context))
 78→                        .anonymizeIp(context.resources.getBoolean(R.bool.ga_anonymizeIp))
 79→                )
 80→                .withHttpClient(OkHttpClientImpl(gaConfig))
 81→                .build()
 82→        }
 83→        installDefaultExceptionHandler()
 84→        val userPrefs = context.sharedPrefs()
 85→        optIn = userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
 86→        userPrefs.registerOnSharedPreferenceChangeListener { sharedPreferences: SharedPreferences, key: String? ->
 87→            if (key == ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY) {
 88→                val newValue = sharedPreferences.getBoolean(key, false)
 89→                Timber.i("Setting analytics opt-in to: %b", newValue)
 90→                optIn = newValue
 91→            }
 92→        }
 93→        return sAnalytics
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    private fun getAnalyticsTag(context: Context): String? {
 97→        if (sAnalyticsTrackingId == null) {
 98→            sAnalyticsTrackingId = context.getString(R.string.ga_trackingId)
 99→        }
100→        return sAnalyticsTrackingId
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun getAnalyticsSamplePercentage(context: Context): Int {
104→        if (sAnalyticsSamplePercentage == -1) {
105→            sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = context.resources.getInteger(R.integer.ga_sampleFrequency)
106→        }
107→        return sAnalyticsSamplePercentage
108→    }
109→
110→    fun setDevMode() {
111→        Timber.d("setDevMode() re-configuring for development analytics tagging")
112→        sAnalyticsTrackingId = "UA-125800786-2"
113→        sAnalyticsSamplePercentage = 100
114→        reInitialize()
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * We want to send an analytics hit on any exception, then chain to other handlers (e.g., ACRA)
119→     */
120→    @Synchronized
121→    private fun installDefaultExceptionHandler() {
122→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = Thread.getDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()
123→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler { thread: Thread?, throwable: Throwable ->
124→            sendAnalyticsException(throwable, true)
125→            if (thread == null) {
126→                Timber.w("unexpected: thread was null")
127→                return@setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler
128→            }
129→            sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler!!.uncaughtException(thread, throwable)
130→        }
131→    }
132→
133→    /**
134→     * Reset the default exception handler
135→     */
136→    @Synchronized
137→    private fun unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler() {
138→        Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler(sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler)
139→        sOriginalUncaughtExceptionHandler = null
140→    }
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Allow users to enable or disable analytics
144→     */
145→    @set:Synchronized
146→    private var optIn: Boolean
147→        get() {
148→            Timber.d("getOptIn() status: %s", sOptIn)
149→            return sOptIn
150→        }
151→        private set(optIn) {
152→            Timber.i("setOptIn(): from %s to %s", sOptIn, optIn)
153→            sOptIn = optIn
154→            sAnalytics!!.flush()
155→            sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled = optIn
156→            sAnalytics!!.performSamplingElection()
157→            Timber.d("setOptIn() optIn / sAnalytics.config().enabled(): %s/%s", sOptIn, sAnalytics!!.config.isEnabled)
158→        }
159→
160→    /**
161→     * Set the analytics up to log things, goes to hit validator. Experimental.
162→     *
163→     * @param dryRun set to true if you want to log analytics hit but not dispatch
164→     */
165→    @Synchronized
166→    fun setDryRun(dryRun: Boolean) {
167→        Timber.i("setDryRun(): %s, warning dryRun is experimental", dryRun)
168→    }
169→
170→    /**
171→     * Re-Initialize the analytics provider
172→     */
173→    @Synchronized
174→    fun reInitialize() {
175→        // send any pending async hits, re-chain default exception handlers and re-init
176→        Timber.i("reInitialize()")
177→        sAnalytics!!.flush()
178→        sAnalytics = null
179→        unInstallDefaultExceptionHandler()
180→        initialize(AnkiDroidApp.instance.applicationContext)
181→    }
182→
183→    /**
184→     * Submit a screen for aggregation / analysis.
185→     * Intended for use to determine if / how features are being used
186→     *
187→     * @param object the result of Object.getClass().getSimpleName() will be used as the screen tag
188→     */
189→    @KotlinCleanup("rename object")
190→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`: Any) {
191→        sendAnalyticsScreenView(`object`.javaClass.simpleName)
192→    }
193→
194→    /**
195→     * Submit a screen display with a synthetic name for aggregation / analysis
196→     * Intended for use if your class handles multiple screens you want to track separately
197→     *
198→     * @param screenName screenName the name to show in analysis reports
199→     */
200→    fun sendAnalyticsScreenView(screenName: String) {
201→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsScreenView(): %s", screenName)
202→        if (!optIn) {
203→            return
204→        }
205→        sAnalytics!!.screenView().screenName(screenName).sendAsync()
206→    }
207→
208→    /**
209→     * Send a detailed arbitrary analytics event, with noun/verb pairs and extra data if needed
210→     *
211→     * @param category the category of event, make your own but use a constant so reporting is good
212→     * @param action   the action the user performed
213→     * @param value    A value for the event, Integer.MIN_VALUE signifies caller shouldn't send the value
214→     * @param label    A label for the event, may be null
215→     */
216→    fun sendAnalyticsEvent(category: String, action: String, value: Int? = null, label: String? = null) {
217→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsEvent() category/action/value/label: %s/%s/%s/%s", category, action, value, label)
218→        if (!optIn) {
219→            return
220→        }
221→        val event = sAnalytics!!.event().eventCategory(category).eventAction(action)
222→        if (label != null) {
223→            event.eventLabel(label)
224→        }
225→        if (value != null) {
226→            event.eventValue(value)
227→        }
228→        event.sendAsync()
229→    }
230→
231→    /**
232→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis, parsed from the exception information
233→     *
234→     * @param t     Throwable to send for analysis
235→     * @param fatal whether it was fatal or not
236→     */
237→    fun sendAnalyticsException(t: Throwable, fatal: Boolean) {
238→        sendAnalyticsException(getCause(t).toString(), fatal)
239→    }
240→
241→    @KotlinCleanup("convert to sequence")
242→    fun getCause(t: Throwable): Throwable {
243→        var cause: Throwable?
244→        var result = t
245→        while (null != result.cause.also { cause = it } && result != cause) {
246→            result = cause!!
247→        }
248→        return result
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Send an exception event out for aggregation/analysis
253→     *
254→     * @param description API limited to 100 characters, truncated here to 100 if needed
255→     * @param fatal       whether it was fatal or not
256→     */
257→    fun sendAnalyticsException(description: String, fatal: Boolean) {
258→        Timber.d("sendAnalyticsException() description/fatal: %s/%s", description, fatal)
259→        if (!sOptIn) {
260→            return
261→        }
262→        sAnalytics!!.exception().exceptionDescription(description).exceptionFatal(fatal).sendAsync()
263→    }

265→    internal fun canGetDefaultUserAgent(): Boolean {
266→        // #5502 - getDefaultUserAgent starts a WebView. We can't have two WebViews with the same data directory.
267→        // But ACRA starts an :acra process which does not terminate when AnkiDroid is restarted. https://crbug.com/558377
268→
269→        // if we're not under the ACRA process then we're fine to initialize a WebView
270→        return if (!ACRA.isACRASenderServiceProcess()) {
271→            true
272→        } else {
273→            hasSetDataDirectory()
274→        }
275→
276→        // If we have a custom data directory, then the crash will not occur.
277→    }

279→    // A listener on this preference handles the rest
280→    var isEnabled: Boolean
281→        get() {
282→            val userPrefs = AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs()
283→            return userPrefs.getBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, false)
284→        }
285→        set(value) {
286→            // A listener on this preference handles the rest
287→            AnkiDroidApp.instance.sharedPrefs().edit {
288→                putBoolean(ANALYTICS_OPTIN_KEY, value)
289→            }
290→        }

297→    /**
298→     * An Android-specific device config generator. Without this it's "Desktop" and unknown for all hardware.
299→     * It is interesting to us what devices people use though (for instance: is Amazon Kindle support worth it?
300→     * Is anyone still using e-ink devices? How many people are on tablets? ChromeOS?)
301→     */
302→    private class AndroidDefaultRequest : DefaultRequest() {
303→        fun setAndroidRequestParameters(context: Context): DefaultRequest {
304→            // Are we running on really large screens or small screens? Send raw screen size
305→            try {
306→                val size = DisplayUtils.getDisplayDimensions(context)
307→                this.screenResolution(size.x.toString() + "x" + size.y)
308→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
309→                Timber.w(e)
310→                // nothing much to do here, it means we couldn't get WindowManager
311→            }
312→
313→            // We can have up to 20 of these - there might be other things we want to know
314→            // but simply seeing what hardware we are running on should be useful
315→            this.customDimension(1, Build.VERSION.RELEASE) // systemVersion, e.g. "7.1.1"  for Android 7.1.1
316→            this.customDimension(2, Build.BRAND) // brand e.g. "OnePlus"
317→            this.customDimension(3, Build.MODEL) // model e.g. "ONEPLUS A6013" for the 6T
318→            this.customDimension(4, Build.BOARD) // deviceId e.g. "sdm845" for the 6T
319→
320→            // This is important for google to auto-fingerprint us for default reporting
321→            // It is not possible to set operating system explicitly, there is no API or analytics parameter for it
322→            // Instead they respond that they auto-parse User-Agent strings for analytics attribution
323→            // For maximum analytics built-in report compatibility we will send the official WebView User-Agent string
324→            try {
325→                if (canGetDefaultUserAgent()) {
326→                    this.userAgent(WebSettings.getDefaultUserAgent(context))
327→                } else {
328→                    this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
329→                }
330→            } catch (e: RuntimeException) {
331→                Timber.w(e)
332→                // Catch RuntimeException as WebView initialization blows up in unpredictable ways
333→                // but analytics should never be a show-stopper
334→                this.userAgent(System.getProperty("http.agent"))
335→            }
336→            return this
337→        }
338→    }

28→
29→        // NONE OF THESE SHOULD BE CHANGED OR A USER WILL LOSE THE ASSOCIATED PREFERENCES

31→        // Deletes are acceptable if the binding should no longer exist
32→        assertEquals(
33→            "binding_SHOW_ANSWER, binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE1, " +
34→                "binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE2, binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE3, binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_EASE4, " +
35→                "binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_RECOMMENDED, binding_FLIP_OR_ANSWER_BETTER_THAN_RECOMMENDED, " +
36→                "binding_UNDO, binding_EDIT, binding_MARK, binding_BURY_CARD, " +
37→                "binding_SUSPEND_CARD, binding_DELETE, binding_PLAY_MEDIA, " +
38→                "binding_EXIT, binding_BURY_NOTE, binding_SUSPEND_NOTE, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_RED, " +
39→                "binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_ORANGE, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_GREEN, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_BLUE, " +
40→                "binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_PINK, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_TURQUOISE, binding_TOGGLE_FLAG_PURPLE, " +
41→                "binding_UNSET_FLAG, binding_PAGE_UP, binding_PAGE_DOWN, binding_TAG, binding_CARD_INFO, binding_ABORT_AND_SYNC, " +
42→                "binding_RECORD_VOICE, binding_REPLAY_VOICE, binding_TOGGLE_WHITEBOARD, binding_CLEAR_WHITEBOARD, " +
43→                "binding_CHANGE_WHITEBOARD_PEN_COLOR, binding_SHOW_HINT, binding_SHOW_ALL_HINTS, binding_ADD_NOTE",
44→            names
45→        )
46→    }

 13→package com.ichi2.anki.analytics
 14→
 15→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 16→import org.hamcrest.MatcherAssert.assertThat
 17→import org.hamcrest.Matchers.*
 18→import org.junit.Assert
 19→import org.junit.Test
 20→import org.junit.experimental.runners.Enclosed
 21→import org.junit.runner.RunWith
 22→import org.junit.runners.Parameterized
 23→import java.lang.RuntimeException
 24→import java.util.*
 25→import kotlin.Throws
 26→import kotlin.reflect.full.memberProperties
 27→import kotlin.reflect.jvm.javaField
 28→
 29→/**
 30→ * This class contains two nested classes and is using the concept of Enclosed runner that internally works as a Suite.
 31→ * The first inner class is a Parameterized class and will run according to the size of test case, while the second
 32→ * inner class is non-parameterized and will run only once.
 33→ */
 34→@RunWith(Enclosed::class)
 35→@KotlinCleanup("auto IDE lint")
 36→@KotlinCleanup("remove JavaField accessors and check for annotations on the property")
 37→@KotlinCleanup("Remove @Throws")
 38→object AnalyticsConstantsTest {
 39→    private val listOfConstantFields: MutableList<String> = ArrayList()
 40→
 41→    init {
 42→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened HelpDialogBox")
 43→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Using AnkiDroid")
 44→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Get Help")
 45→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Support AnkiDroid")
 46→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Community")
 47→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened AnkiDroid Manual")
 48→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Anki Manual")
 49→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened AnkiDroid FAQ")
 50→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Mailing List")
 51→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Report a Bug")
 52→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Donate")
 53→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Translate")
 54→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Develop")
 55→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Rate")
 56→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Other")
 57→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Send Feedback")
 58→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Anki Forums")
 59→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Reddit")
 60→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Discord")
 61→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Facebook")
 62→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Twitter")
 63→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened Privacy")
 64→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened AnkiDroid Privacy Policy")
 65→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened AnkiWeb Privacy Policy")
 66→        listOfConstantFields.add("Opened AnkiWeb Terms and Conditions")
 67→        listOfConstantFields.add("Exception Report")
 68→        listOfConstantFields.add("Import APKG")
 69→        listOfConstantFields.add("Import COLPKG")
 70→        listOfConstantFields.add("Import CSV")
 71→        listOfConstantFields.add("Tapped setting")
 72→        listOfConstantFields.add("Changed setting")
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    internal val analyticsConstantFields
 76→        get() =
 77→            UsageAnalytics.Actions::class.memberProperties
 78→                .filter { x -> x.javaField!!.getAnnotation(AnalyticsConstant::class.java) != null }
 79→                .also { list -> assertThat(list.size, not(equalTo(0))) }
 80→
 81→    @RunWith(Parameterized::class)
 82→    class AnalyticsConstantsFieldValuesTest(private val analyticsString: String) {
 83→        /**
 84→         * The message here means that the string being checked cannot be found in Actions class.
 85→         * If encountered with this message, re-check the list present here and constants in Actions class, to resolve
 86→         * the test failure.
 87→         */
 88→        @Test
 89→        @Throws(IllegalAccessException::class)
 90→        fun checkAnalyticsString() {
 91→            Assert.assertEquals(
 92→                "Re-check if you renamed any string in the analytics string constants of Actions class or AnalyticsConstantsTest.listOfConstantFields. If so, revert them as those string constants must not change as they are compared in analytics.",
 93→                analyticsString,
 94→                getStringFromReflection(analyticsString)
 95→            )
 96→        }
 97→
 98→        @Throws(IllegalAccessException::class)
 99→        fun getStringFromReflection(analyticsStringToBeChecked: String): String? {
100→            for (value in analyticsConstantFields) {
101→                val reflectedValue = value.get(UsageAnalytics.Actions)
102→                if (reflectedValue == analyticsStringToBeChecked) {
103→                    return reflectedValue as String
104→                }
105→            }
106→            return null
107→        }
108→
109→        companion object {
110→            @JvmStatic // required for Parameters
111→            @Parameterized.Parameters
112→            fun addAnalyticsConstants(): List<String> {
113→                return listOfConstantFields
114→            }
115→        }
116→    }
117→
118→    class AnalyticsConstantsFieldLengthTest {
119→        @Test
120→        fun fieldSizeEqualsListOfConstantFields() {
121→            if (fieldSize > listOfConstantFields.size) {
122→                Assert.assertEquals(
123→                    "Add the newly added analytics constant to AnalyticsConstantsTest.listOfConstantFields. NOTE: Constants should not be renamed as we cannot compare these in analytics.",
124→                    listOfConstantFields.size,
125→                    fieldSize
126→                )
127→            } else if (fieldSize < listOfConstantFields.size) {
128→                Assert.assertEquals(
129→                    "If a constant is removed, it should be removed from AnalyticsConstantsTest.listOfConstantFields. NOTE: Constants should not be renamed as we cannot compare these in analytics.",
130→                    listOfConstantFields.size,
131→                    fieldSize
132→                )
133→            } else {
134→                Assert.assertEquals(listOfConstantFields.size, fieldSize)
135→            }
136→        }
137→
138→        /**
139→         * This test is used to check whether all the string constants of Actions are annotated with @AnalyticsConstant.
140→         * If not, then a runtime exception is thrown.
141→         */
142→        @Test
143→        fun fieldAnnotatedOrNot() {
144→            for (value in getProperties()) {
145→                if (value.getAnnotation(AnalyticsConstant::class.java) == null && !value.isSynthetic) {
146→                    throw RuntimeException("All the fields in Actions class must be annotated with @AnalyticsConstant. It seems " + value.name + " is not annotated.")
147→                }
148→            }
149→        }
150→
151→        private fun getProperties() = UsageAnalytics.Actions::class.memberProperties
152→            .mapNotNull { it.javaField }
153→            .also { list -> assertThat("fields should not be empty", list.size, not(equalTo(0))) }
154→
155→        companion object {
156→            /**
157→             * This method is used to get the size of fields in Actions Class.
158→             * Because whenever a new constant is added in Actions Class but not added to the list present in this
159→             * class (listOfConstantFields) the test must fail.
160→             */
161→            val fieldSize get() = analyticsConstantFields.count()
162→        }
163→    }
164→}
